column1: # redis complexity ladder — LABEL<TAB>PROMPT, escalating reading breadth

column2: Where is the `dictAdd` function defined? Give the file and line.
Where is `dictAdd` defined, and list every call site across the source tree with file and line.
Trace how a SET command is executed in Redis: name every function it passes through from the moment the command is dispatched to where the value is stored in the keyspace, in order.
Explain how Redis key expiration works end to end. Identify the functions that set a TTL, lazily expire a key on access, and actively expire keys in the cron, and describe how they call each other.

Give an architectural overview of how a write command propagates from a master to its replicas in Redis: name every function involved on both the master side (command propagation, replication backlog, feeding replicas) and the replica side (receiving and applying the stream), and how they connect.
